home.title: Hryhorii Kyrylchenko
home.subtitle: C# .NET Developer | Web Applications
home.description: Driven by curiosity and a commitment to lifelong learning, I embrace challenges and craft solutions that make a difference — every project is an opportunity to grow, adapt, and deliver real value. I’m dedicated to creating applications that bring real value to people’s lives.

about.title: Hi, I'm Hryhorii Kyrylchenko
about.description: Aspiring .NET developer with a diverse professional background in law, logistics, sales, and consulting. Completed training at IT STEP Academy in Poland, proficient in C#, ASP.NET Core, SQL, and more. Seeking new challenges in .NET development.
about.content:
I am a beginner .NET developer with an unconventional professional background in areas such as law, logistics, sales, and consulting. This rich experience has shaped skills like analytical thinking, adaptability, and communication—valuable additions to my technical knowledge.

After moving to Poland, I successfully completed my studies at IT STEP Academy, where I mastered C#, ASP.NET Core, Web API, SQL, EF Core, LINQ, as well as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. This provided me with a solid foundation for transitioning into web development.

Currently, I am open to interesting projects and positions in the .NET development field—aiming to apply my skills effectively and grow together with a team.

cookies.page_description: This policy explains how cookies are used on this site.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.p1: This website hyrhoriikyrylchenko.dev (the "Site") uses cookies and similar technologies to ensure proper functioning. All data stored—such as cookies and local storage—is strictly necessary for the operation of this developer portfolio site.

what-are.title: What are Cookies?
what-are.p1: Cookies are small text files stored in your browser to enable key functionalities like sessions and language or theme settings. Local storage also preserves preferences such as "theme" (light/dark).

usage.title: How We Use Cookies
usage.p1: We use minimal, essential storage mechanisms only.

types.title: Cookie Types
types.p1: Essential cookies and storage: Necessary for site operation. These include browser-set cookies like “translocoLang”, as well as “cookie_consent” and local storage key “theme”. Without them, the site would not function properly. Consent is assumed through use of the site.

manage.title: How to Manage Cookies
manage.p1: These essential cookies and settings cannot be disabled without impacting the site's functionality. You may clear them via your browser settings, but the site may not work correctly afterward.

changes.title: Changes
changes.p1: This policy may be updated. Please revisit this page for any changes.

cookies.title: We use cookies
cookies.start: to improve your experience on our site.
cookies.end: By continuing to use our site, you agree to this.

privacy.page_description: This policy explains how this website processes data and protects user privacy.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.p1: This Privacy Policy explains how the website hyrhoriikyrylchenko.dev (the "Site") handles information when you visit it. This Site is a static developer portfolio and does not actively collect personal data from users.

data_controller.title: Data Controller
data_controller.p1: The data controller for this website is the owner of hyrhoriikyrylchenko.dev. If you have any questions regarding this Privacy Policy, you may contact the website owner using the contact information provided on the Site.

personal_data_collection.title: Personal Data Collection
personal_data_collection.p1: This Site does not provide user accounts, contact forms, newsletters, or any interactive features that would require the submission of personal information.
personal_data_collection.p2: The Site does not intentionally collect, store, or process personal data such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, or payment information.

technical_data.title: Automatically Processed Technical Data
technical_data.p1: When you access the Site, certain technical information may be processed automatically by the hosting provider. This may include IP address, browser type, device information, and access timestamps.
technical_data.p2: Such data is processed solely for the purpose of ensuring server security, preventing abuse, and maintaining proper website operation. The website owner does not directly access or use this information for tracking or profiling purposes.

cookies_storage.title: Cookies and Local Storage
cookies_storage.p1: The Site uses only essential cookies and browser storage mechanisms necessary for its operation.
cookies_storage.p2: These may include a language preference cookie (such as "translocoLang"), a cookie consent indicator ("cookie_consent"), and a local storage key for theme preference ("theme").
cookies_storage.p3: These storage mechanisms do not track users, are not used for advertising purposes, and do not share data with third parties.

third_parties.title: Third-Party Services
third_parties.p1: The Site does not use third-party analytics tools, advertising networks, tracking pixels, or marketing integrations.
third_parties.p2: The website may be hosted by a third-party hosting provider, which may process technical data as part of its infrastructure services.

data_sharing.title: Data Sharing
data_sharing.p1: The website owner does not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer personal data to third parties.
data_sharing.p2: No personal data is shared for marketing, profiling, or commercial purposes.

data_retention.title: Data Retention
data_retention.p1: Because the Site does not collect personal data directly, no personal data is stored or retained by the website owner.
data_retention.p2: Technical server logs, if generated by the hosting provider, are retained according to the hosting provider’s internal policies.

user_rights.title: User Rights
user_rights.p1: Under applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), users may have rights regarding their personal data.
user_rights.p2: Since this Site does not directly collect or process personal data, most data protection rights may not apply in practice. However, users may contact the website owner with any privacy-related inquiries.

security.title: Data Security
security.p1: The Site uses standard security practices provided by the hosting provider to protect against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction of data.

changes.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
changes.p1: This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time to reflect legal or technical changes. Any updates will be published on this page.

contact.title: Contact
contact.p1: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, you may contact the website owner using the contact details provided on the Site.

forja.title: Forja – Independent Game Studio Platform
forja.short: A robust, identity-driven content platform tailored for game development builds, player feedback, and team content management.

sections.results: Delivered a high-performance backend within a focused 3-month sprint. My role included designing the database schema, structuring and implementing the backend architecture, all backend functionality, and integrating related technologies. Agile teamwork ensured seamless coordination with frontend, design, and security specialists.
sections.features: Player feedback mechanisms, bug reporting tools, and an internal content management system for organizing studio projects, and content assets.
sections.overview: Forja is a bespoke platform developed for an independent game studio, intentionally steering clear of generic solutions. It offers a streamlined environment for managing game builds, collecting player feedback, and overseeing team content, all within a cohesive ecosystem.
sections.deployment: The platform is designed for deployment on cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS, Azure, or any private cloud). With Docker-based containerization, the system can be easily scaled, orchestrated, and maintained in cloud environments, enabling streamlined release workflows and infrastructure management.
sections.architecture: The backend is constructed using ASP.NET Core (C#), serving over 40 dynamic UI pages via controllers and services. Data is managed with PostgreSQL, while Redis is employed for caching to enhance performance. MinIO is utilized for scalable object storage, and Keycloak manages authentication. API documentation is maintained using Swagger, and the entire system is orchestrated through the .NET Aspire Dashboard.
sections.technologies: C#, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core, Docker, Git, Keycloak, PostgreSQL, Redis,  MinIO,  Swagger, .NET Aspire Dashboard

personal-site.title: Personal Developer Portfolio & Blog
personal-site.short: A modern, multilingual full‑stack site with portfolio, blog, and admin panel, showcasing development projects and professional skills.

sections.results: I developed the entire site, including backend, frontend, and database, implementing responsive design and multilingual support, and collaborating with a designer for UX/UI.
sections.features: Portfolio with project showcases, blog section, About page, contact form with email notifications, social links, admin panel for content management, and localization via JSON and database translations. Unit tests implemented with xUnit, Moq, and FluentAssertions.
sections.overview: This full-stack site features an 'About' section, portfolio pages with detailed projects, a blog with articles, and a contact form. It demonstrates development skills, project showcases, and professional branding with dynamic interaction and multilingual support.
sections.deployment: Containerized with Docker, CI/CD implemented via GitHub Actions, deploying to AWS. Includes automated database migrations and infrastructure provisioning for reliable deployment and updates.
sections.architecture: Backend uses Domain-Driven Design and Clean Architecture with Vertical Slice patterns. MediatR implements CQRS, separating commands and queries. Each vertical slice contains its own request/handler logic, validation, and endpoint definitions. Frontend is Angular, fully responsive and localized.
sections.technologies: C#, TypeScript, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core, Angular, PostgreSQL, Postman, Git, GitHub Actions, AWS, HTML, CSS, Rider, WebStorm.

portfolio-static.title: Personal Portfolio (Static Version)
portfolio-static.short: A static version of my personal portfolio website, showcasing projects and skills.

sections.results: This is a static copy of my fullstack portfolio site. It shows projects, skills, and frontend capabilities. Backend, dynamic blog, and admin panel are not included.
sections.features: Supports static project listings, multilingual support (en/pl/ru/uk), and basic site navigation. All content is loaded from local JSON files. No backend API, authentication, or dynamic content.
sections.overview: The static site is built with Angular and deployed to GitHub Pages as a Single Page Application (SPA). It demonstrates my frontend skills, projects portfolio, and personal branding.
sections.deployment: Deployed using ng build --configuration production and npx gh-pages -d dist/portfolio-static. No server or backend required.
sections.architecture: Frontend-only SPA using Angular standalone components. No SSR, backend, or admin panel is included. The focus is on a lightweight, static showcase of the original project.
sections.technologies: Angular, TypeScript, SCSS, GitHub Pages, JSON for static content.

frameworks.name: Frameworks
frameworks.description: Frameworks and libraries I work with

csharp.name: C#
csharp.description: A modern, general-purpose, object-oriented programming language by Microsoft, running on .NET and used for web, desktop, mobile, and game development.

typescript.name: TypeScript
typescript.description: A statically typed superset of JavaScript by Microsoft, adds optional types and tooling for scalable, maintainable applications; compiles to plain JavaScript.

javascript.name: JavaScript
javascript.description: A high-level, multi-paradigm, dynamically typed scripting language that powers interactivity on the web, running in browsers and on servers like Node.js.

aspnetcore.name: ASP.NET Core
aspnetcore.description: A high-performance, cross-platform, open-source framework for building modern web applications and APIs using .NET.

entityframeworkcore.name: Entity Framework Core
entityframeworkcore.description: A lightweight, cross-platform ORM by Microsoft enabling data access via .NET objects instead of raw SQL; supports migrations, LINQ, and multiple database providers like PostgreSQL.

angular.name: Angular
angular.description: An open-source, TypeScript-based framework by Google for building scalable single-page applications with integrated routing, forms, HTTP, and modular architecture.

blazor.name: Blazor
blazor.description: Declarative web UI framework from Microsoft using C# and Razor components to build SPA and interactive applications running on Server or WebAssembly.

grpc.name: gRPC
grpc.description: Open-source RPC framework using HTTP/2 and Protocol Buffers for efficient, strongly-typed service communication.

aws.name: AWS
aws.description: Cloud platform by Amazon offering on-demand compute, storage, databases, analytics, and over 200 services.

css.name: CSS
css.description: Style sheet language defining presentation of HTML documents including layout, colors, fonts, and responsive design.

docker.name: Docker
docker.description: Open-source platform for automating application deployment inside lightweight containers across environments.

git.name: Git
git.description: Distributed version control system tracking source code changes and enabling collaborative development.

html.name: HTML
html.description: Standard markup language for structuring web pages using elements like headings, paragraphs, links, and images.

keycloak.name: Keycloak
keycloak.description: Open-source identity and access management solution providing single sign-on, user federation, and fine-grained authorization.

minio.name: MinIO
minio.description: High-performance open-source object storage system compatible with AWS S3 for cloud-native and scalable environments.

postman.name: Postman
postman.description: Comprehensive API platform for designing, testing, and managing APIs.

postgresql.name: PostgreSQL
postgresql.description: Open-source object-relational database known for reliability, extensibility, and SQL standards compliance.

rider.name: Rider
rider.description: Cross-platform .NET IDE by JetBrains based on IntelliJ and ReSharper, supporting C#, ASP.NET, JavaScript, TypeScript, SQL, and more.

swagger.name: Swagger
swagger.description: Suite of open-source tools for designing, documenting, and consuming REST APIs based on the OpenAPI specification.

webstorm.name: WebStorm
webstorm.description: Cross-platform IDE by JetBrains for JavaScript and TypeScript with advanced code intelligence and debugging tools.

githubactions.name: GitHub Actions
githubactions.description: Integrated CI/CD platform in GitHub enabling automated build, test, and deployment workflows triggered by repository events.

redis.name: Redis
redis.description: An in-memory data structure store used as a database, cache, and message broker for fast data access and real-time processing.
